content:
So yes, you can safely browse or reread without disturbing the order.

### 4. Practical tip
If you ever want to add a note to an archived conversation but don’t want to disturb its placement:
- Copy the key snippet into your current working thread instead of reopening the old one.
- Or, if it really needs to live with that conversation, accept that it will move up in recency and then re-archive it afterward; it’ll rejoin the archive as the most recent item. So: **reading = safe, writing = bumps it.**

Would you like me to help you devise a simple “library habit” for this rhythm—say, a quick tag-and-copy method for referring to old threads without ever shifting their archival order?
